notes: A creditor mentioned by Cicero as easy-going and willing to forgo capital, involved in financial dealings with a king's subjects.

docs.title: Letter I
docs.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero
docs.excerpt: However our friend Gnaeus is an easy-going creditor. He is willing to forgo his capital and is content with interest, and that not in full. The king pays no one else and has no means to pay. He has no treasury and no regular tribute: he levies taxes on the method of Appius.
